steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: In B(t) = 70(2)^t, the input t is time in days and the output B(t) is the predicted yeast count. B(5) = 2,240 means: plug in t = 5, get 2,240. The question asks which choice correctly labels which value is the time and which is the count.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 5 days after the number of yeast cells was first recorded, the predicted number of yeast cells was 2,240.: t = 5 days is the input, B(5) = 2,240 cells is the output. This matches the function correctly. ✓
  • Choice B — 5 days after the number of yeast cells was first recorded, the predicted number of yeast cells was 32.: Uses the correct input t = 5 days, but claims the output is 32 cells. B(5) = 70 · 25 = 2,240, not 32. ✗
  • Choice C — 2,240 days after the number of yeast cells was first recorded, the predicted number of yeast cells was 5.: Swaps t and B(t) — 2,240 is the output, not the time. ✗
  • Choice D — 2,240 days after the number of yeast cells was first recorded, the predicted number of yeast cells was 32.: Also swaps the roles; 32 has no basis in B(5) = 2,240. ✗
That is the correct answer.
